title: Tcl/Tk Cheat Sheet
description: Essential Tcl/Tk commands and patterns for engineers.

commands.description: Prints a string to the console.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to output text to the console for debugging or display.

commands.description: Assigns a value to a variable.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to store data in a variable for later use.

commands.description: Executes commands based on a condition.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to perform conditional logic in your script.

commands.description: Defines a new procedure with arguments.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to create reusable blocks of code with parameters.

commands.description: Executes commands repeatedly while a condition is true.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to loop through code until a condition changes.

commands.description: Iterates over each element in a list.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to process each item in a list individually.

commands.description: Creates a button widget in a Tk application.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to add interactive buttons to a GUI application.

commands.description: Packs a widget into the window in a Tk application.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to arrange widgets in a Tk window.

commands.description: Creates a list from given elements.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to group multiple values into a single list.

commands.description: Evaluates an expression and returns the result.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to perform arithmetic operations or evaluations.
Use when you need to verify the existence of a file before processing it.

commands.description: Executes a command after a delay.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to schedule a command to run after a certain time.

commands.description: Catches errors in a block of code and stores the error message.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to handle errors gracefully in your script.
